checks.a_analysis: The output document faithfully follows the text of the source PDF. All headings and body paragraphs correspond to content found in the original document. No invented sections, fabricated table-of-contents entries, or hallucinated body text were found.

The phone number '(919) 807-4781' was correctly extracted and matches the source. No fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs were detected. While one email address was omitted from the text, no incorrect or non-existent contact data was added to the output.

Content from both pages of the source PDF is present in the output. All main functional sections identified on the two pages (e.g., Representation, Funding, Planning on page 1; BAS, LUCA, PSAP, CCC, GSS/SDRP on page 2) are represented in the structured JSON.
The source document does not contain any formal tables. Data is presented in text blocks and infographics, which the conversion pipeline has correctly handled as paragraphs, lists, and images.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The email address for the primary contact person, 'Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov', is missing from the contact information section at the end of Page 2. In the source, the email appears between the organization name and the phone number. In the output, element 27 (organization) is followed immediately by element 29 (phone number), with the email address completely omitted. This is a significant loss of information for a contact block.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 2, contact information block (between element reading_order 27 and 29)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording of the extracted text blocks matches the source. The bolding of specific terms in the 'By taking a few minutes...' paragraph on Page 1 is an acceptable change as it reflects the visual emphasis (blue bold text) used in the original document.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text or sections. Each content block from the source PDF is represented once in the JSON structure.

In Section 2 (Page 2), the heading hierarchy skips a level. The main title of the second page is assigned H1 ('Preparing for the Census – We Have Work To Do!'), but the next heading in that section is 'Bob Coats', which is assigned H3. This skips the H2 level entirely, which is a structural defect according to the provided rules.
checks.b_evidence_source: Heading: Preparing for the Census – We Have Work To Do! and Bob Coats
checks.b_evidence_output: {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "Preparing for the Census – We Have Work To Do!"} ... {"type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "Bob Coats"}

checks.a_analysis: There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

The reading order for the second page is materially incorrect regarding the timeline image (reading_order 30). In the source PDF, the timeline graphic appears at the very top of the page, immediately following the title, and provides the chronological context for the subsequent text. In the output JSON, this image is placed at the very end of the document, following the contact footer (reading_order 29). This significantly disrupts the logical flow of information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual timeline graphic located at the top of page 2.

checks.a_analysis: The segments for BAS, LUCA, and PSAP on page 2 are converted into an unordered list. While the source uses bold paragraph headers rather than a formal list, this is a valid structural interpretation that maintains the meaning and relationship of the items.

The email address 'Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov' present in the contact information footer on page 2 of the source document is completely missing from the output. This is a loss of contact data visible in the source.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text block containing the email is missing between reading_order 27 and 29.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the multi-page PDF into a single continuous web page. The multi-column sidebars from page 1 ('Representation', 'Funding', 'Planning') are successfully converted into sequential single-column sections, which is appropriate for responsive HTML layout. The infographic elements and stats sections are preserved in a way that maintains the original logical flow and organization of information. A user can easily find and understand the same information as presented in the source PDF.

The source document does not contain traditional grid-based tables. It uses graphic layouts for statistics and timelines. These elements are rendered as images or formatted text/images in the HTML, remaining perfectly readable and understandable. No broken table structures were identified.

All meaningful visual elements from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the North Carolina flag map logo, the official Census 2020 logos, the statistical icons (people, house, calendar, target), the multi-year timeline graphic, and the program funnel infographic. All images render correctly without broken links or missing components.

There is no genuine duplication of body content. While some text from graphics (like the timeline and funnel infographic) is repeated as plain text below the respective images, this serves as a text alternative for accessibility and does not constitute a defect of content duplication. The presence of headings in both the Table of Contents and the body is an expected navigation feature. No paragraphs or sections are repeated within the body content itself.
